title: Stafuv Rahz
content:

**[[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]]<ref name="rank and uniform">This is implied by the color of his uniform, which is colored black like NCOs within the [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]], as well as his holding the rank of Major, which within the Imperial Military is used for the Imperial Army and the Stormtrooper Corps</ref>

{{Quote|<s></s>''Fools!'' When ''Major Rahz'' receives an order from ''Lord Vader''…it gets ''executed!''|Stafuv Rahz|<ref name="bmtc" />}}
'''Stafuv Rahz''' served as a [[Major/Legends|major]] in the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. A [[Gektl]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] from [[Hoszh Iszhir]] who later betrayed his own people for power as an Imperial, Rahz maintained his [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] [[Planetary shield/Legends|shield system]], which was used to protect the world from greedy magnates hoping to tap the planet's valuable natural resources. Imbued with vaulting ambition, Rahz effectively sold the freedom of his species for his own gain when he offered [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] a way to bypass Hoszh Iszhir's planetary shield. Palpatine accepted, and while the Gektls of Hoszh Iszhir were [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] and put to work as [[Miner/Legends|miners]], Rahz served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] and, more directly, [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]].

Following a [[Raid on Kar'a'katok|raid]] on [[Kar'a'katok]], which resulted in the recruitment of [[Suprema|Ampotem Za]], future overlord of [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore]], Rahz was ordered by Vader to kidnap the teacher [[Myoris Lin]] and her students on the planet [[Harix]]. Rahz did so, but one student, [[Berd Lin|Berd]], managed to escape his clutches. The escapee found asylum with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], and worked against Rahz to free his captive classmates and his mother, Lin. In the ensuing skirmish, Rahz's fleet was destroyed, but the Gektl attempted to save face by executing his prisoners. In attempting to utilize a [[sonic beam projector]] against the captives, Stafuv Rahz was [[Death/Legends|killed]], as the weapon had been sabotaged by Berd.

==Biography==
===Ascension===
Born and raised on inhospitable [[Hoszh Iszhir]], Stafuv Rahz worked to maintain the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] [[Planetary shield/Legends|defense shields]], which were used to ward away any prospectors that might try and [[Mining/Legends|mine]] the planet's valuable [[Guerrerite/Legends|guerrerite]] reserves. Rahz, caring naught for his fellow [[Gektl]]s, traveled to [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial Center]] hoping for an audience with [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. He was granted one, and in his audience with the head of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], he offered to supply Palpatine with the codes to bypass Hoszh Iszhir's shields in exchange for a commission in the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] and control over his native [[Star system/Legends|star system]].<ref name="aite" />

Palpatine agreed to the terms, as he was eager to extract and utilize the valuable natural resources of Hoszh Iszhir. He had a fleet of [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] bombard the planet and then enslaved the Gektls, putting them to work in the guerrerite mines. Rahz was assigned to [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], who despised the Gektl for his dishonest and traitorous ways and made no attempt to hide his opinion of Rahz. Some time after his appointment, Rahz was sent to [[Kar'a'katok]] to lead a [[Raid on Kar'a'katok|raid]] on the long-secluded planet. Rahz's men subdued and captured [[Suprema|Ampotem Za]], one of the planet's native [[Shimholt]]. Za managed to escape captivity and return to Kar'a'katok, but was shunned by his fellow Shimholt, as the planet's government had barred off-world travel by the natives over four thousand years prior. Now an outcast, Za returned to Rahz's custody. The Gektl took Za before Palpatine, who installed the Shimholt as overlord of [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]].<ref name="aite" />

===Harix plot===
{{Quote|<s></s>''Take the teacher alive!''<s></s>|Stafuv Rahz|<ref name="bmtc" />}}
[[File:RahzBurn.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Rahz receives instructions from Vader.]]
In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], Rahz was dispatched to [[Harix]] to kidnap a teacher, [[Myoris Lin]], and her students. Lin was suspected of being sympathetic to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], and Vader hoped that by holding her and the children hostage, they could lure her Rebel contacts into a trap. Lin attempted to herd her students though an escape channel in a desperate attempt to flee Rahz's clutches, but the Gektl was able to capture them nonetheless. However, one student, Lin's son Berd, was able to escape and was rescued by Rebel Alliance member [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], who had been hoping to deliver a series of illegal pre-[[New Order/Legends|New Order]] [[micro-book]]s.<ref name="aite" />

Skywalker, departing Harix, destroyed a small [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of [[Starship/Legends|ships]] Rahz sent after him, prompting the Gektl to request aid and rescue from Lord Vader.<ref name="aite" /> Returning to the Dark Lord's [[Command ship/Legends|command ship]], Rahz, along with his colleagues, was given the order to broadcast the news that the hostage students would be executed if the Rebels did not show themselves. Vader also made it clear that this was Rahz's last chance at being rescued, and any further requests for rescue from him would be his last for any place in the galaxy. Rahz was then ordered to organize and oversee the executions personally, and to have a fleet of his [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighters]] lie in wait behind the [[Talhu Slotted Peaks]] of Harix in preparation for the rescue attempt Vader predicted.<ref name="bmtc" />

===Death===
{{Quote|<s></s>''For obstructing Empire justice…you will all di-i-i-i—|Stafuv Rahz's final words|<ref name="bmtc" />}}
[[File:RahzLiteralBurn.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Rahz perishes due to the modifications of Berd.]]
As he had predicted, Vader was soon met with the sight of a thousand enemy ships approaching his base.<ref name="bmtc" /> As Rahz and the Imperials prepared their defenses,<ref name="aite" /> the Gektl's entire fleet of TIE Fighters was destroyed in an avalanche of rubble from [[Shaelo Cliff]] that had been caused by Skywalker and his ally [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. Aghast at the loss of his fleet and the sudden communications jamming he and his men found themselves under, Rahz hoped to save face by executing his prisoners with the [[sonic beam projector]] he had been preparing.<ref name="bmtc" /> Before he could carry out the execution, he was attacked by Skywalker, who began grappling with the desperate Gektl.<ref name="aite" />

The attacking "fleet" was actually nothing more than a group of [[Deep Space Target Practice Unit]]s that had been altered to resemble spacecraft, which the Rebels had used as a distraction against the Imperials while they attempted to save the captive children.<ref name="aite" /> The real fleet began to swoop over the Lins' school, unloading scores of [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] who had been touched by young Berd's plight. Rahz, who was being held by his adversaries, shed his tail, skin, and clothes in desperation, fought his way out of captivity, and made his way towards the sonic beam projector. Before the Gektl could fire it at his enemies, however, Berd rephased the power of the weapon, inadvertently causing it to destroy Rahz instead.<ref name="bmtc" />

==Personality and traits==
[[File:MajorWallyGator-CSWTEA7.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Rahz struggles to elude capture]]
An ambitious Gektl, Stafuv Rahz had no qualms in harming others in the pursuit of his own gains. Bearing no patriotism for his native Hoszh Iszhir, the Gektl effectively gave up the freedom of his entire race in exchange for an Imperial uniform. This was likely born of the native Gektl instinct to survive—Hoszh Iszhir being such an inhospitable place, dominated by barren deserts, vicious predators, and poisonous clouds, its inhabitants would always do their utmost to stay alive. Rahz, however, went beyond his natural means in achieving this, although it earned him the ire of his superiors.<ref name="aite" /> Despite this, he was always eager to impress Lord Vader, and was constantly fearful of the Dark Lord.<ref name="bmtc" />

==Equipment==
Owing to his becoming part of the Empire's officers, Rahz wore a black Imperial uniform similar to those of the NCOs of the Stormtrooper Corps, albeit with shorts and lacking boots or gloves due to his Gektl appendages.<ref name="bmtc" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Believe it or not, I commented on this name to Rich about three years ago, and he told me, "Say it aloud." So I tried it, and I thought, "Stuff ... Staff UV Rays? What the hell does that mean?" My Indy license was immediately revoked.|Abel G. Peña, on the ''Indiana Jones'' reference in Rahz's full name|<ref name="boards">{{JCFCite|url=posts/27723870|board=Literature|thread=Aliens of the Empire (Part 2 now up)|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|date=2007-11-10|time=4:07 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304014652/http://boards.theforce.net/threads/aliens-of-the-empire-part-2-now-up.27707732/page-3}}</ref>}}
Major Rahz first appeared in [[Russ Manning]]'s [[Star Wars (comic strip)|comic strip]] ''[[Bring Me the Children]]'', published by the [[Los Angeles Times Syndicate]] in [[1980]].<ref name="bmtc" /> The character's first name of Stafuv was later established by [[Abel G. Peña]] and [[Rich Handley]] in their online supplement ''[[Aliens in the Empire]]'', published on [[StarWars.com]] in the [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] subscription feature in [[2007]].<ref name="aite" /> Rahz's first name was a reference to the "[[w:c:indianajones:Headpiece to the Staff of Ra|Staff of Ra]],"<ref name="boards" /> an artifact from the [[1981]] [[Indiana Jones (franchise)|''Indiana Jones'']] film [[Wikipedia:Raiders of the Lost Ark|''Raiders of the Lost Ark'']],<ref name="raiders">[[Wikipedia:Raiders of the Lost Ark|''Raiders of the Lost Ark'']]</ref> which ''[[Star Wars]]'' creator [[George Lucas]] both conceived and executive produced.<ref name="George Lucas">''[[George Lucas: The Creative Impulse]]''</ref> Peña did not initially pick up on the reference in the character's name when discussing it with Handley around [[2004]].<ref name="boards" />
